items.title: Attack on ship in Hormuz leads UN to halt evacuation plan for trapped sailors
items.summary: MANAMA: An attack on a ship Thursday in the Strait of Hormuz led the UN to suspend efforts to evacuate trapped mariners as the United States warned against Iran charging fees for passage through the vital waterway. The New York Times and other US media reported that Iran struck a container ship in the strait, and the Iranian agency that claims to regulate traffic there issued a warning after the incident. "Any passage through routes outside the framework designated by PGSA will not be covered by safe passage guarantees," Persian Gulf Strait Authority said on X. Iran imposed a blockade of the strait during the war, sparking a global economic shock, and has since said it plans to introduce what it terms maritime service fees.

items.title: Road rage driver filmed attacking motorist in Melaka walks away after both sides make peace
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — A road rage incident that saw a Proton Saga driver repeatedly punch and kick another motorist at a Melaka traffic junction has been resolved amicably after both parties agreed not to pursue the matter further, according to police. Melaka Tengah acting district police chief Superintendent Halim Abas said both the complainant and the suspect had been called in to give their statements. “The complainant and the suspect reached a mutual understanding and reconciled amicably.

items.title: Police probe fake teacher scam targeting parents with bogus school payment QR code
items.summary: IPOH, June 25 — The police received two reports regarding the actions of an unidentified individual impersonating a teacher, allegedly collecting payments for school academic programmess from parents using a fake quick response (QR) code. Manjung district police chief ACP Hasbullah Abd Rahman said the Commercial Crime Investigation Division of the Manjung district police headquarters (IPD) received a report yesterday regarding the modus operandi used by the suspect, and further investigations are underway. “The suspect also sent a modified payment QR code using the school’s name to convince the victims to make the payment to the suspect’s account,” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: Baby girl dies after collapsing at babysitter’s home, woman detained to assist probe
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, June 26 — An 11-month-old baby girl died today after she was allegedly injured when she fell from a chair while being fed at her babysitter’s house in Bedong on June 22. Kuala Muda police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said the baby was pronounced dead at 10.42 am while receiving treatment at Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital (HSB) in Alor Setar. Hanyan said police had detained a woman in her 30s to assist in the investigation, with the case being investigated under Section 31 of the Child Act 2001.

items.title: Fadhlina: Advocacy on Child Act, Anti-Bullying Act to be rolled out in schools
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Advocacy programmes on the Child Act 2001, the Anti-Bullying Act 2026 and the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017 will be rolled out across the education sector. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the initiative was agreed upon during a meeting today with a delegation from the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam), led by Children’s Commissioner Dr Farah Nini Dusuki and Dr Mohd Al Adib Samuri. Fadhlina said collaboration between the Education Ministry and Suhakam should be strengthened to raise awareness at all levels while ensuring a safe learning environment for children.

items.title: 'Alligator Alcatraz' detention centre shuts in US
items.summary: OCHOPEE, United States: The controversial "Alligator Alcatraz" immigration detention centre – a costly Florida facility that became a symbol of US President Donald Trump's deportation drive – has shut down after less than a year in operation, officials said Thursday. Florida governor Ron DeSantis, appearing at the remote Everglades site with White House border czar Tom Homan, said the facility no longer held any detainees and had fulfilled the emergency role it was built to serve. "Alligator Alcatraz fulfilled the role that it was designed to serve," DeSantis said, adding that it had helped remove "many, many dangerous people" from Florida and the United States.

items.title: KPDN seizes 1.36 tonnes of suspected diverted subsidised cooking oil in Kelantan
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 26 — The Kelantan branch of the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Living Costs (KPDN) seized 1.36 tonnes of subsidised cooking oil, suspected of being diverted, in a coordinated operation in Pasir Mas on June 24. Its director, Hanif Yusabra Yusuf, said that the seizure was carried out through Ops Tiris 4.0 (Integrated) as a result of intelligence and monitoring conducted by the Special Intelligence and Action Unit of Kelantan KPDN, in collaboration with the Marine Police Force Region Three Pengkalan Kubor branch, the Pasir Mas district police headquarters and the Pasir Mas KPDN. He said that intelligence led the operation team to track a locally registered Perodua Alza vehicle for nearly 10 kilometres, starting from a control goods wholesaler premises in Pasir Mas.

items.title: Bowler dies after collapsing during tournament at Kuala Selangor shopping centre
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 26 — A man died after he was believed to have collapsed while competing in a bowling tournament at a shopping centre in Kuala Selangor on Wednesday. Kuala Selangor police chief Supt Azaharudin Tajudin said police received a report from the victim’s younger brother at 12.15 am yesterday after he was informed by the deceased’s friend about his brother’s condition on June 24. “The 36-year-old victim was rushed to Tanjong Karang Hospital for treatment but was pronounced dead at 10.12 pm.

items.title: Driver dies after suspected heart attack leaves Proton Saga overturned on its roof in Kota Tinggi
items.summary: He died at the scene, police said. Kota Tinggi police chief Supt Yusof Othman said the single-vehicle crash occurred at about 2.45am along KM5.1 Jalan Kota Tinggi-Kluang (Jalan Tun Habab). The case is still under police investigation,” he said.

items.title: Fadillah hails PAAB’s two decades of strengthening Malaysia’s water security
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Pengurusan Aset Air Berhad (PAAB) has grown into one of the key pillars in ensuring that the development of the country’s water sector is carried out in a more structured, sustainable and effective manner over the two decades since its establishment. Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof said that throughout that period, the PAAB has played an important role in strengthening the development of the nation’s water assets and infrastructure. Commenting on water infrastructure development, Fadillah, who is also Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation, stressed that it is a long-term investment whose benefits are not always immediately visible.

items.title: Two killed in Lenggong crash: Police officer's car loses control while overtaking
items.summary: LENGGONG: The crash that killed a police officer and a teacher at Km29 of Jalan Kuala Kangsar–Baling near Restoran Tasik Raban yesterday occurred after a car driven by the officer lost control while overtaking. Gerik police chief Supt Abdul Samad Othman said preliminary investigations found that the Toyota Vios was believed to have veered into the opposite lane while travelling towards Kuala Kangsar. "The vehicle then collided with a Honda Odyssey carrying six female teachers travelling towards Gerik ," he said in a statement last night.

items.title: Man dies after being flung 30m from SPE bridge as SUV crashes into sheltering motorcyclists
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — A motorcyclist was killed after being flung about 30 metres from a bridge in a crash involving a sport utility vehicle (SUV) and two motorcycles at KM15.8 of the Sungai Besi-Ulu Kelang Expressway (SPE) here yesterday afternoon. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said in the incident, which occurred at about 4.40 pm, a woman driving a Proton X50 SUV was believed to have lost control of the vehicle during heavy rain before skidding and crashing into two motorcycles that had stopped for shelter. “Another motorcyclist and his pillion rider, both aged 18, sustained facial injuries and are receiving treatment at Kuala Lumpur Hospital,” he said in a statement last night.

items.title: PAS says time to move forward after Johor seat talks with Umno, Parti Wawasan stall
items.summary: MUAR, June 25 — PAS is looking ahead and does not want to prolong the issue of seat distribution involving the party, Parti Wawasan Negara (Wawasan) and Umno ahead of the upcoming Johor state election, its vice-president Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar said. Also present were Perikatan Nasional (PN) information chief and Johor state election director Tan Sri Annuar Musa, and Johor PAS commissioner Datuk Mahfodz Mohamed. On Wednesday, the announcement of Barisan Nasional (BN) candidates for the Johor state election in Johor Bahru was postponed several times.

items.title: Azalina: Petronas fuel supply expected to remain stable until August
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Fuel supplies across the nationwide network of Petronas petrol stations are expected to remain stable and sufficient until August, said Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform), Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said. “This network accounts for approximately 50 percent of the country’s domestic fuel requirements, supplied by Petronas through its publicly listed subsidiary, Petronas Dagangan Bhd,” she added. Accordingly, she said that to ensure adequate national fuel supplies and coordinate the government’s efforts in addressing the global energy crisis arising from the conflict in West Asia, a Crisis Management Task Force (PPPK) has been established under MTEN.

items.title: PAS fields ex-Johor Bersatu chief Mazlan Bujang in 11-candidate state poll line-up
items.summary: MUAR, June 26 — Former Johor executive councillor Mazlan Bujang, once the state chief of Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu), is among 11 candidates fielded by PAS to represent Perikatan Nasional (PN) in the Johor state election. PN chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar announced that the 61-year-old will contest the Kahang state seat. Ahmad Samsuri also announced nine new faces among the 11 candidates who will contest the upcoming Johor state election.

items.title: Three kiosks destroyed in Petaling Street Market fire
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Three kiosks at Petaling Street Market along Jalan Hang Lekir were destroyed in an early morning fire today. The Kuala Lumpur Fire and Rescue Department's operations centre received an emergency call at 12.21am and dispatched firefighters from the Hang Tuah station, assisted by crews from Pudu and Titiwangsa. Kuala Lumpur Fire and Rescue Department operations commander Logeswaran Lavan said the blaze completely destroyed all three kiosks.

items.title: King Charles breaks precedent to reveal £30mil paid in taxes since 2022
items.summary: LONDON: King Charles III has paid more than £30 million (US$39.6 million) in personal taxes since becoming monarch in September 2022, Buckingham Palace announced Thursday, in the first such disclosure by a reigning British sovereign. That comprised £11.7 million in 2023-24 and £12.9 million in 2024-25. He stumped up £8.34 million in 2023-24 and £7.76 million in the 2024-25 tax year.

items.title: Bersatu names 16 Johor election candidates with ex-deputy Speaker Rashid Hasnon, former MB Dr Sahruddin leading line-up
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 26 — Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) has announced its list of 16 candidates under the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition for the upcoming Johor state election. Former Dewan Rakyat deputy Speaker Datuk Rashid Hasnon and former Johor menteri besar Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal headline the list. Rashid, the former Batu Pahat MP, will contest the Senggarang state seat for PN.

items.title: New York City freezes rents for one million apartments in Mayor Mamdani victory
items.summary: NEW YORK: A New York City housing board voted yesterday to freeze the rents for about one million regulated apartments for up to two years, fulfilling a central campaign promise of Mayor Zohran Mamdani only months into his term. The 7-1 vote by the city's Rent Guidelines Board set increases at zero on both one-year and two-year leases from October. "This is a historic victory for New York City tenants," Mamdani said in a statement.

items.title: Fuel supply at Petronas stations secure until August – Azalina
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Fuel supplies across Petronas petrol stations nationwide are expected to remain stable and sufficient until August. She said the Petronas network accounted for about 50 per cent of the country's domestic fuel supply through its listed subsidiary, Petronas Dagangan Bhd. To safeguard national fuel supplies and coordinate the government's response to the global energy crisis arising from the Middle East conflict, she said a Crisis Management Task Force had been established under NEAC.

items.title: Torrential rain from passing typhoon shuts down southern Taiwan, rail line cut
items.summary: TAIPEI, (Taiwain): Torrential rains from a passing typhoon shut down a swathe of southern Taiwan on Friday, leaving more than five million people off work or school and flooding severing a section of a main rail line. Although Typhoon Mekkhala, which is now over southern Japan's Ryukyu Islands, did not make direct landfall in Taiwan, its outer bands have brought heavy rain to parts of the island, especially in Kaohsiung, Tainan and Pingtung in the south. Severe flooding in Tainan shut down a section of the main north-south railway line, thought the separate high speed rail line reported no problems.

items.title: Wall St ends mixed as tech megacap declines outweigh upbeat chip outlook
items.summary: The Nasdaq closed lower on Thursday, dragged down by losses in Big Tech shares, while the S&P closed near flat and the Dow closed higher as investors digested new economic data. Shares of Nvidia, Microsoft, and Alphabet were also down between 0.5 per cent and 3.5 per cent. Memory chipmaker Sandisk also soared 22 per cent.

items.title: Advocacy on Child Act, anti-bullying law and sexual offences to be rolled out
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Advocacy programmes on the Child Act 2001, the Anti-Bullying Act 2026 and the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017 will be rolled out across the education sector. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the initiative was agreed upon during a meeting yesterday with a Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) delegation led by Children's Commissioner Dr Farah Nini Dusuki and Dr Mohd Al Adib Samuri. Fadhlina said the Education Ministry and Suhakam would strengthen collaboration to raise awareness at all levels while ensuring a safe learning environment for children.

items.title: German court jails 12 members of Nigerian 'love scam' network
items.summary: BERLIN: A German court jailed 12 members of a Nigerian organised crime ring on Thursday for their part in so-called "love scams" and money laundering. The court in Munich said those convicted were aged between 34 and 55 and were sentenced to prison terms ranging from three years and four months to eight-and-a-half years. The court said the victims were mostly "women, elderly people or people with disabilities."

items.title: Analysts upbeat on Kee Ming's RM70m solar job, sees strong earnings contribution
items.summary: Kenanga Research said the RM70 million subcontract is expected to generate about RM14 million in gross profit over a 249-day contract period, assuming a conservative gross margin of 20 per cent. It said this implies potential interconnection works opportunities worth about RM833 million, for which this project win has accounted for RM70 million. Following the win, Kenanga Research has raised its order-book replenishment assumptions to RM270 million for FY27 (from RM230 million) and RM350 million for FY28 (from RM310 million), reflecting stronger growth prospects in the domestic utility-scale solar landscape.

items.title: CIMB sees strong earnings growth for Axiata, reinitiates coverage with 'Buy' call
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: CIMB Securities Sdn Bhd has resumed coverage on Axiata Group Bhd with a "Buy" rating and a target price of RM2.55. CIMB Securities said Axiata's valuation remains attractive, with its FY27 enterprise value to earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortisation (EV/EBITDA), including associates, of 5.4 times at a 16 per cent discount to its five-year mean. "Correspondingly, we estimate Axiata may receive RM4.3 billion (47 sen per share) in cash, which could reduce its end-FY26 net debt/Ebitda to just 0.9 times from 2.5 times.

items.title: CGS keeps 'Add' call on YTL Power, sees stronger data centre-driven earnings outlook
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: CGS International maintained its "Add" call on YTL Power International Bhd with an unchanged target price of RM4.60, citing the group's improving earnings quality, stronger revenue visibility and increasing contribution from long-term data centre contracts. The research house said YTL Power is accelerating the expansion of its Johor data centre campus, supported by strong customer demand, faster construction timelines and plans to raise total capacity at its Kulai green data centre park to 1.2 gigawatts (GW) from 600 megawatts (MW). YTL Power is targeting 500 MW of committed capacity by end-2027 and has begun construction of an additional 200 MW across its JDC5 and JDC11 facilities.

items.title: The value of pay transparency
items.summary: Effective leadership is built on trust. For this reason, leaders should be transparent about how pay decisions are made and explain clearly why some individuals earn more than others. Rather, it means creating clarity around the principles, standards and expectations that determine compensation.

items.title: France, Italy want to set up multinational coalition to succeed UNIFIL in Lebanon
items.summary: ANTIBES, France: France and Italy want to set up a multinational coalition to succeed the UN peacekeeping force in Lebanon, UNIFIL, French President Emmanuel Macron said on Thursday. The new force would help prevent Lebanon's territory from becoming "a foothold for regional escalation," Macron said. "Italy and France can absolutely make a difference," the Italian prime minister said.

items.title: Israel says no timeframe for troop withdrawal from Lebanon, Gaza and Syria
items.summary: JERUSALEM: Israel said Thursday that it had set no timetable for withdrawing its forces from Lebanon, Gaza and Syria, as Israeli and Lebanese officials engaged in US-brokered talks in Washington. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said at the same function that troops would remain in south Lebanon "as long as necessary." Under US pressure, Lebanese officials began direct talks in April with Israel in Washington.

items.title: Mindef rolls out five-year defence strategic plan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Defence Ministry (Mindef) yesterday launched two landmark strategic documents outlining the nation's defence action plan for the next five years: the National Defence Strategic Plan (PSPN) 2026–2030 and the Defence Capacity Blueprint (RTKP) 2026 –2030 . He said the PSPN 2026–2030 will continue to support the Defence White Paper, anchored on seven pillars. Khaled said the launch of the PSPN 2026–2030 was crucial to national defence, reflecting the government's commitment to strengthening confidence in defence management.

items.title: The ghost of 2022: Will voter apathy again haunt Johor’s July 11 election?
items.summary: When Johor last voted in March 2022, it witnessed a dismal turnout of just 54.92 per cent. In 2018, turnout stood at a robust 84.51 per cent with over 1.5 million votes. For context: Sarawak state election (2021): 60.67 per cent Sabah state election (2025): 64.35 per cent Melaka state election (2021): 65.85 per cent Negeri Sembilan state election (2023): 68.35 per cent Selangor state election (2023): 71.98 per cent Penang state election (2023): 72.67 per cent Kedah state election (2023): 73.86 per cent Terengganu state election (2023): 74.79 per cent 15th General Election (2022): 74.70 per cent Whether the numbers rebound this time will likely decide the fate of the next government.

items.title: NRD to use up old MyKad stock first
items.summary: APPLICATIONS for the new MyKad have been overwhelming since June 1, the National Registration Department (NRD) said. NRD director-general Datuk Badrul Hisham Alias, however, said the department would first use its remaining stock of old MyKad before rolling out the upgraded version. Saifuddin said new MyKad applications would not immediately receive the upgraded version, as the government planned to clear its remaining stock before rolling out the high-security cards.

items.title: MPI secures RM1mil support
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: The Malaysian Press Institute (MPI) has secured RM1.037 million in sponsorships and contributions for Malaysian Press Night 2026 (MWM 2026). The total includes RM450,000 from Petronas for the MPI-Petronas Malaysian Journalism Awards 2025 cash prizes and RM587,000 in contributions from 60 organisations. MPI chief executive officer Dr Ainol Amriz Ismail said: "The support received... reflects a collective commitment to strengthen professional, ethical and trusted journalism in the country."

items.title: Sim: Ministry will continue to empower domestic firms
items.summary: "Our mission is to strengthen Malaysian businesses," he said at the launch of the ready-to-drink (RTD) collaboration between Tealive and Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd here yesterday. Tealive and Dutch Lady launched their dairy milk tea range, MooTea and MeowCha. Dutch Lady Malaysia managing director Veronika Utami said the partnership reflects the company's commitment to innovation and its ambition to make dairy relevant across more consumption occasions.

items.title: Trio offer clues to longevity
items.summary: RIO DE JANEIRO: What is the secret to a long life? Findings from the three sisters' case could help scientists better understand why some people remain physically and cognitively resilient at exceptionally advanced ages. The sisters, Zulina de Deus Nunes, 103, Zoraide de Deus Mota, 104, and Levita de Deus Nunes, 109, who live in Rio de Janeiro, were identified through LongeviQuest, a global organisation that verifies longevity records and partners with Guinness World Records.

items.title: New Rembau Undang installation to be formalised at Istana Besar Seri Menanti this Saturday
items.summary: KUALA PILAH, June 25 —The Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, has consented to hold the royal audience ceremony to formalise the installation of the Undang of Luak Rembau at Istana Besar Seri Menanti this Saturday. The royal decree was conveyed by Tunku Besar Seri Menanti, Tunku Ali Redhauddin Tuanku Muhriz, during an audience with the Datuk-Datuk Adat of Rembau at Istana Besar Seri Menanti today. “I wish to convey the consent of my father, Tuanku Muhriz, for the Istiadat Menghadap Menjunjung Duli Bagi Menyempurnakan Kejadian Undang Luak Rembau.

items.title: Cost-cutting measures not meant to halt official travel, but to manage rising subsidy costs, says MOF
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 25 — The government’s ongoing cost-cutting measures are not intended to halt all official travel or government spending, but rather constitute a targeted fiscal management approach to absorb rising subsidy costs following the increase in global oil prices, the Ministry of Finance (MOF) said in a statement today. “To help finance part of these additional subsidy costs, MOF has issued guidelines on government cost-cutting measures. “Among the measures implemented is restricting official overseas travel to only scheduled commitments, mandatory meetings, and matters that are truly important and have a strategic impact on the country,” the statement said.

items.title: Ex-Petronas manager leaked confidential data to Petros, Cyber Security unit confirms in court
items.summary: Former Petronas senior manager of Corporate Relations Jumsuri Basri, 48, testified that he was informed on December 6, 2024, at the Petronas Twin Towers, that Mohd Khairul Akmal Mohd Jasni had leaked the confidential information. The first prosecution witness said checks revealed that at 3.19pm on June 8, 2024, Mohd Khairul Akmal had sent a confidential document titled “Q1 2024 Upstream Business Performance” via his Petronas email address to a Petros email address. “Checks by Petronas Cyber Security confirmed that Mohd Khairul Akmal had sent the document to Petros on June 8, 2024.

items.title: Prisons (Amendment) Bill 2026 deferred, referred to two parliamentary select committees for scrutiny
items.summary: Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar Nasarah said the Bill will be referred to the PSSC on Security and the PSSC on Human Rights, Election and Institutional Reform for further scrutiny. “The Home Ministry has taken note of all the issues raised during the debate on this Bill, and we are carefully considering the views and feedback presented,” he said when winding up the debate on the bill in the Dewan Rakyat today. Shamsul Anuar said that, in general, both committees had responded positively to the amendments proposed by the Home Ministry through the Bill.

items.title: Govt drafting new law to tighten e-commerce platform accountability, plug regulatory gaps
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — The government is drafting a new legal framework for e-commerce platforms to strengthen platform accountability, address regulatory gaps and better protect local micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs). Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the study on the proposed legislation began in April 2024, with the final report now available on the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living’s (KPDN) website. Armizan said the proposed legislation was prompted by regulatory gaps between local businesses and foreign cross-border sellers, with the aim of creating a fairer competitive environment, strengthening platform accountability and tackling counterfeit products.

items.title: Govt says border control officers safe in service scheme transition, assures no loss of benefits
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — The government has assured that the welfare and rights of personnel of the Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) will remain protected during the transition to a new service scheme under the Public Service Department (PSD), which takes effect on July 1. Deputy Home Minister Datuk Seri Dr Shamsul Anuar Nasarah said no officer would be affected in terms of promotion prospects, seniority, retirement benefits or welfare if they choose to remain under their original service scheme within their respective agencies. “Positions within AKPS were filled through secondments from these core agencies prior to the implementation of the new service scheme.

items.title: Elephants under watch in Japan as Malaysia tracks welfare of trio sent to Tennoji Zoo
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — The Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability (NRES) is closely monitoring the status and welfare of three Malaysian Asian elephants sent to Tennoji Zoo in Osaka, Japan, in March. Deputy Minister Syed Ibrahim Syed Noh said the relocation of the three elephants, named Dara, Amoi and Kelat, forms part of a 25-year strategic ex-situ conservation cooperation programme between Taiping Zoo and Night Safari (ZTNS) and Tennoji Zoo. “To ensure the welfare of these elephants remains optimal throughout their acclimatisation phase, seven Perhilitan mahouts have accompanied the animals on a rotational basis for two months since March.

items.title: PSM fields lone candidate in Johor state election, targets Skudai seat
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 25 — Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) has named Amir Syafiq Ameer Soekre as its sole candidate for the Johor state election, where he will contest the Skudai state seat. PSM deputy chairperson S. Arutchelvan said the decision to field only one candidate in the Johor polls was made due to the high cost of elections and to allow the party to focus its efforts on a constituency with the potential to make an impact on its political struggle. As such, PSM has chosen to contest a strategic constituency where it believes it can make the greatest impact,” he told a press conference introducing the party’s candidate for the Johor state election today.

items.title: Johor top cop: 153 police reports lodged nationwide over Puad Zarkashi’s alleged remarks
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 25 — Johor police have confirmed that 153 police reports have been lodged nationwide as of this afternoon regarding a statement by Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi alleging interference by the Palace in the dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly. Johor police chief CP Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the reports were lodged by various parties, including a former Johor state executive councillor and the political secretary to the Johor Menteri Besar. “Investigations are being conducted under several legal provisions, namely Section 4(1) of the Sedition Act 1948, Section 505(b) of the Penal Code and Section 233 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: PAC flags hospital billing practices, warns of drivers behind rising medical inflation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — The Parliamentary Accounts Committee (PAC) has raised concerns over private hospital billing practices and pricing structures, warning that they are contributing to rising medical inflation and higher insurance premiums. In a report tabled in Parliament, the bipartisan panel said private hospital billing systems lack transparency and may not accurately reflect actual costs, with medicines and supplies reportedly marked up by as much as 300 per cent to cross-subsidise operational expenses. “The PAC found the practice of unbundling charges for basic items such as clinical waste disposal, pillowcases, and alcohol swabs, which should have been included in the room charges,” Kapar MP Dr Halimah Ali, a PAC member, said when reading the report in Parliament.

items.title: EC tells Johor candidates: Early form checks, deposit payments key to smooth nominations
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 25 — Prospective candidates for the 16th Johor State Election are advised to pre-check their nomination forms at the Returning Officer’s Office or the State Election Office to ensure a smooth nomination process this Saturday. Election Commission (EC) secretary Datuk Khairul Shahril Idrus said all nomination papers must be submitted at 56 nomination centres, which will be open from 9am to 10am. In addition, he said, candidates are also encouraged to make their deposit payments early and bring along the payment receipt as proof when submitting their nomination papers.

items.title: Azalina urges Umno unity, tells members to back party choices after Johor candidate tensions
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 25 — Umno members have been urged to place loyalty to the party’s struggle, principles, and the public above personal disappointment following the candidate selection process for the 16th Johor state election. Umno Information chief Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the party’s maturity and discipline required all factions to accept the decisions made and move forward together, despite dissatisfaction following the candidate nominations. Azalina also praised Johor Umno Liaison Committee chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi and his leadership team, crediting them with handling a difficult selection process with calm, discipline, and political courage.

items.title: Johor Umno Youth files police report against Puad Zarkashi over alleged 3R remarks
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 25 — The Johor Umno Youth has lodged a police report against Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi over a statement allegedly touching on 3R issues (race, religion and royalty) and feared to have the potential to generate dissatisfaction and hostility towards the Royal Institution. Johor Umno Youth chief Noor Azleen Ambros said the statement by the former Rengit state assemblyman and Umno Supreme Council member also contained elements of incitement that could lead to public unrest. He said Umno Youth divisions in all 26 divisions across the state would also lodge police reports on the matter today.

items.title: Thunderstorm alert for KL and nine states until 8pm, MetMalaysia warns of heavy rain and winds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued an alert for severe thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds across Kuala Lumpur and nine states until 8pm tonight. In a statement today, MetMalaysia indicated that the severe weather is expected to hit Perlis, Kedah, Perak (Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Mualim) and Terengganu (Kuala Nerus, Hulu Terengganu, Kuala Terengganu, Marang and Dungun). In Selangor, the affected areas include Hulu Selangor, Gombak, and Petaling, while Kluang, Mersing, and Kota Tinggi in Johor are also on alert.

items.title: Merdeka Center survey: KL mayor election proposal wins majority support as reform drive gains traction
items.summary: Support was stronger among non-Malay respondents, where 65 per cent were in favour and only 10 per cent opposed. Among Malay respondents, 56 per cent supported the proposal while 26 per cent were against it. This includes 71 per cent support among Malay respondents and 75 per cent among non-Malay respondents.

items.title: Anwar remains Malaysia’s most popular leader, with Khairy close behind as economy tops voter concerns, Merdeka Center survey finds
items.summary: Among opposition figures, former prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin registered 36 per cent approval. He is followed by newly appointed Perikatan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar at 28 per cent, PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang at 25 per cent, and Umno president and Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi at 24 per cent. Overall, federal government approval stands at 50 per cent satisfied versus 48 per cent dissatisfied.
